mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) or negative mean during rate cut periods over a 3-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 55% of triggered instances or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real personal income and housing starts indicate weakening aggregate demand that suppresses corporate earnings growth, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint declines fail to predict negative returns with a Sharpe ratio below -0.5 over 60-day horizons in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth signals expanding manufacturing capacity and rising corporate output, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days during economic recovery phases.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns do not materialize during recovery phases or the signal shows negative predictive power (Rank IC < -0.01) in this regime.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly inflation readings above 3.0% trigger expectations of restrictive monetary policy that increase discount rates and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in > 60% of high-inflation months or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening that supports consumer spending and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce positive 60-day returns with statistical significance (p > 0.10) or shows negative correlation with forward returns in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP reflects expanding economic activity that improves corporate profit expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no positive correlation with GDP growth or the Rank IC remains below 0.02 across all rolling 2-year evaluation windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production signals weakening manufacturing output that reduces corporate revenue forecasts and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days during late-cycle expansions.
failure_condition: Negative returns do not materialize during late-cycle phases or the signal yields positive forward returns in > 50% of triggered instances over a 3-year walk-forward test.
